climate: Warm subtropical climate with hot, humid summers and mild, pleasant winters.
description: Sydney is Australia's largest city, home to the iconic Sydney Opera House and Harbour Bridge, and one of the world's most liveable cities. It hosts world-class universities including the University of Sydney and UNSW, has a well-established Chinese community, and Chinese restaurants and Asian supermarkets can be found throughout the city. Abundant internship and employment opportunities make it a top destination for international students.

climate: Temperate oceanic climate with four distinct seasons and changeable weather — you can experience all four seasons in a single day.
description: Melbourne has been ranked as one of the world's most liveable cities for many consecutive years, renowned for its vibrant coffee culture, street art, and multiculturalism. Prestigious universities such as the University of Melbourne and Monash University are located here, creating a strong academic atmosphere. The city is home to one of Australia's largest Chinese communities, offering convenient living and a rich array of cultural activities.

climate: Subtropical climate with abundant sunshine, warm and humid summers, and dry, mild winters.
description: Brisbane is the capital of Queensland and will host the 2032 Olympics, with broad prospects for urban development. The University of Queensland ranks among the world's top fifty, and the city also hosts quality institutions like QUT. With a pleasant climate and moderate living costs, Brisbane offers excellent value for international students.

climate: Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers and mild, rainy winters, with abundant sunshine year-round.
description: Perth is the largest city on Australia's west coast and shares the same time zone as China, making it easy to stay in touch with family. The University of Western Australia is a world top-100 university, and Curtin University is renowned in engineering. Perth boasts beautiful beaches and a relaxed pace of life, with abundant employment opportunities in the mining sector.

climate: Mediterranean climate with hot, dry summers and cool, wet winters, with four distinct seasons.
description: Adelaide is known as the 'Festival City' and is one of Australia's most affordable capital cities. The University of Adelaide is a member of the Group of Eight with strong academic credentials. The regional migration bonus points policy is particularly attractive to international students, offering better chances of obtaining permanent residency after graduation.

climate: Temperate continental climate with four distinct seasons, warm summers, and cold, dry winters.
description: Canberra is Australia's capital and home to the top-ranked Australian National University (ANU), a world leader in academics. As the political centre, the city has numerous cultural institutions, including the National Library and the War Memorial, which are free to visit. The city is well-planned, safe, and has the highest average income in Australia.

climate: Subtropical climate, warm year-round with plenty of sunshine and brief afternoon thunderstorms in summer.
description: The Gold Coast is world-famous for its 70 kilometres of golden sandy beaches, renowned as a holiday destination and surfing paradise. Bond University and Griffith University's Gold Coast campus provide quality education for international students. The lifestyle here is relaxed and enjoyable, with plenty of sunshine, beaches, and outdoor activities to enjoy outside of class.

climate: Temperate oceanic climate with cool, pleasant summers and cold, wet winters, with distinct scenery in each season.
description: Hobart is Australia's southernmost capital city, known for its stunning natural scenery and tranquil living environment. The University of Tasmania is one of Australia's oldest universities and a world leader in marine and Antarctic research. As a regional city, international students can benefit from additional migration bonus points, offering excellent value.

climate: Tropical climate divided into wet and dry seasons, with high temperatures year-round and monsoon rainfall during the wet season.
description: Darwin is Australia's northernmost capital city and the closest to Asia, with a rich multicultural atmosphere. Charles Darwin University offers unique programs in tropical research and Indigenous culture. As a regional city, it provides significant migration bonus points, and the job market is less competitive.

climate: Temperate oceanic climate, similar to Melbourne, with warm summers and cool, rainy winters.
description: Geelong is Victoria's second-largest city, just one hour's drive from Melbourne, situated on the beautiful Corio Bay. Deakin University's Geelong campus is the city's main higher education institution, with a picturesque campus. Living costs are much lower than Melbourne, and it qualifies for regional migration bonus points, making it ideal for budget-conscious students.

climate: Temperate oceanic climate with warm summers, mild winters, and even rainfall throughout the year.
description: Wollongong is located about 80 kilometres south of Sydney, nestled between mountains and the sea with an outstanding natural environment. The University of Wollongong is one of Australia's top ten research universities, globally recognised for its engineering and computer science programs. Living costs are much lower than Sydney, while easy access to Sydney's big-city resources remains available.

climate: Temperate oceanic climate with warm, humid summers, mild winters, and abundant sunshine.
description: Newcastle is the second-largest city in New South Wales, located about 160 kilometres north of Sydney, with beautiful beaches and a vibrant urban culture. The University of Newcastle is a well-known Australian research university, with particular strengths in medicine and engineering. The city is undergoing a transformation with a thriving startup scene and reasonable living costs.

climate: Tropical climate, warm year-round with distinct wet and dry seasons, and comfortably dry winters.
description: Townsville is the largest city in North Queensland, adjacent to the World Heritage-listed Great Barrier Reef, with an exceptional natural environment. James Cook University is world-renowned for marine biology and tropical research, making it an ideal choice for students in these fields. With low living costs and regional migration bonus points, it suits students seeking a unique experience.

climate: Tropical climate with hot, wet summers and warm, dry winters, suitable for outdoor activities year-round.
description: Cairns is the gateway to the Great Barrier Reef and tropical rainforests, with exceptionally rich tourism resources. James Cook University's Cairns campus offers specialised programs in marine science and tourism management. With a relaxed pace of life and significant regional migration bonus points, it is an ideal destination for nature-loving students.

climate: Temperate climate with four distinct seasons, cold winters, and warm, dry summers.
description: Ballarat is an important historical and cultural city in Victoria that once thrived during the gold rush and retains rich cultural heritage to this day. Federation University offers diverse course options for international students. With very low living costs, a friendly community, and regional migration bonus points, it is well-suited for students who prefer a quiet study environment.
